source.title: Amygdala lesions reduce cataplexy in orexin knock-out mice.

confidence_rationale: Direct lesion-based causal evidence in the canonical orexin-KO narcolepsy model. Bilateral amygdala lesions sharply reduce emotion-triggered cataplexy without disturbing baseline sleep-wake architecture. Anterograde/retrograde tracing maps the projection (CeA GABAergic neurons -> vlPAG, LPT, LC, DRN) -- exactly the brainstem motor-tone targets MECH-281 names. Mapping fidelity to the motor-coupling axis is high; transfer risk from mouse to a generic REE coupling claim is moderate.
failure_signatures: If amygdala (specifically CeA GABAergic) projections to brainstem motor-tone nuclei are intact, emotion-triggered loss of motor activation (cataplexy-analog) persists in orexin-deficient animals -- consistent with MECH-281's prediction that the motor-coupling failure runs through amygdala arbitration rather than through z_harm computation itself.

Lesioning amygdala does not abolish baseline sleep-wake structure -- so a model that conflates the wake-stability axis (MECH-286) with the motor-coupling axis (MECH-281) would over-predict the lesion effect.

mapping.source_claim_statement: GABAergic neurons in the central nucleus of the amygdala project heavily to brainstem nuclei that maintain waking muscle tone (vlPAG, LPT, LC, DRN); bilateral excitotoxic lesions of the amygdala in orexin-KO mice markedly reduce cataplexy events triggered by positive emotional stimuli (wheel running, chocolate) without disturbing baseline sleep-wake architecture.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-281 posits that orexin-analog gain modulation gates the coupling between drive_level / emotional state and motor activation, with BLA/CeA arbitration as one canonical downstream target. Burgess et al. provide the cleanest mechanistic substrate: in orexin loss, the amygdala -> brainstem motor-tone path becomes the dominant cause of emotion-triggered motor failure. The REE counterpart is the override_signal (SD-037) reweighting the amygdala arbitration term in commit-gate threshold computations; when override_signal is at zero, amygdala-driven inhibition of motor activation dominates.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Cataplexy is REM-atonia-related and species-specific (rodent emotional triggers differ from human laughter). The REE claim is broader: any failure of drive/emotion to recruit motor activation. Burgess shows the brainstem-projecting CeA-GABA path is sufficient for emotion-induced atonia in orexin loss; it does not directly demonstrate that the same circuit handles non-atonia motor-coupling failures (e.g., depression-like psychomotor retardation). Treat as strong evidence for the canonical narcolepsy axis, suggestive for the broader claim.
mapping.source_context: Mouse, orexin-KO model, ad libitum cataplexy-trigger paradigm (running wheel, chocolate), excitotoxic and tracing methods.

confidence_components.notes: J Neurosci, well-replicated narcolepsy lab (Scammell). Mapping is direct for the canonical motor-coupling axis; transfer risk is the rodent->primate->architectural-claim chain rather than the mechanism itself.
